strands.title: hamutay Phase 1 EXECUTION — CYCLE 51 SMOKE TEST COMPLETION + FULL EXECUTION READINESS
strands.content: CYCLE 51 INTEGRATION: Phase 1 smoke test protocol COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY. CYCLE 50-51 CHECKPOINT: (1) Turn 151 (uv sync result): UV dependency resolution SUCCESSFUL — all 17 packages resolved and installed, including anthropic==0.84.0, pydantic==2.12.5, hamutay==0.1.0 (from local build). Zero unresolved dependencies, zero version conflicts. (2) Turn 152-153 (log_reader sample parse test): log_reader.read_session_jsonl() SUCCESSFUL on real baseline_run1 session — parsed 55 turns from file /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl WITHOUT errors. Sample output shows correct parsing: Turn 1 (user), Turn 2-4 (assistant with tool_use markers), Turn 5-6 (user tool_result). Content structure validated: turns have turn_number, role (user/assistant), content fields. SMOKE TEST VALIDATION COMPLETE: Infrastructure validated, log_reader works on real data, session file accessible and readable. SESSION SCALE ASSESSMENT: 55 turns = moderate execution time (~15-25 min at Haiku latency + processing). Executable within Cycle 51 budget. NEXT ACTION: PROCEED TO PHASE 1 FULL EXECUTION — run run_projection.py with --max_turns=20 for fast feedback iteration (conservative estimate: first 20 turns ≈ 10-15 min). After completion: assess trajectory sanity (strand stability, token boundedness, loss distribution), then decide Phase 1+ metrics or debug path.

key_claims.text: UV sync (turn 151) completed successfully: 17 packages resolved, no version conflicts, anthropic SDK + pydantic + hamutay installed.
log_reader.read_session_jsonl() smoke test (turn 152-153) PASSED: parsed 55 turns from real baseline_run1 session without errors.
Session file accessibility VERIFIED: file exists at /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl, readable by log_reader.
log_reader correctly identified turn structure: turn_number, role (user/assistant), content fields present; tool_use and tool_result markers handled.
Session scale assessment: 55 turns = moderate execution (~15-25 min), executable in Cycle 51; --max_turns=20 recommended for fast iteration.

strands.title: PICHAY REPLAY MODULE — CYCLE 51 READINESS (READY FOR PARALLEL CODE REVIEW PHASE)
strands.content: CYCLE 51 INTEGRATION: Replay module DEFERRED (Cycle 49-50) until Phase 1 smoke tests completed. NOW UNBLOCKED: Smoke test success (turn 151-153) enables parallel replay module code review phase (Cycle 51 Stage 1). SPECIFICATION CARRIES FORWARD (turn 145): Build src/pichay/replay.py parser + reconstruction + page fault detection + stats aggregation. PREREQUISITES: read pager.py, proxy.py, eval.py to understand proxy JSONL format, pager compaction algorithm, eval stats patterns. CYCLE 51 PLAN: Parallel track: (1) Stage 1 (THIS TURN): Code review of pager.py, proxy.py, eval.py to extract module interfaces, data formats, integration points. (2) Stage 2 (Cycle 51-52): Design replay.py architecture (parser → reconstruction → page fault detector → stats aggregator). (3) Stage 3 (Cycle 52): Implement + test replay.py on proxy logs from baseline_run1. CRITICAL UNKNOWNS (DEFERRED TO CODE REVIEW): (a) proxy.py JSONL schema — what fields are logged at each turn? Full message snapshots or API boundary markers? (b) page fault definition — is it string-match re-request of evicted content? Or semantic detection? (c) pager.py integration — does proxy pipe through pager? Or is pager optional? (d) eval.py stats pattern — what statistics are aggregated (success rates, token counts, page faults, latency)? CARRY-FORWARD: Code review prerequisites remain OPEN until Cycle 51 inspection of source files.

key_claims.text: Replay module now UNBLOCKED for Cycle 51 code review: Phase 1 smoke tests passed, parallel track can proceed.
Replay module prerequisites (proxy schema, page fault def, pager integration) remain OPEN until code review of pager.py, proxy.py, eval.py.
Cycle 51 plan: parallel Phase 1 execution (main) + replay code review (Stage 1); design + implementation deferred to Cycle 51-52.

strands.title: CYCLE 51 DECISION TREE & EXECUTION PATHWAY — FROM SMOKE TEST SUCCESS TO FULL PHASE 1 RUN
strands.content: CYCLE 51 INTEGRATION: Smoke test success (turn 151-153) enables PHASE 1 FULL EXECUTION decision. DECISION LOGIC: Smoke tests (uv sync, log_reader parse) PASSED → PROCEED to Phase 1 full execution immediately. NO BLOCKERS. EXECUTION COMMAND (recommended, conservative): cd /home/tony/projects/hamutay && python -m hamutay.run_projection /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl ./output_tensors/ --max_turns=20. RATIONALE: (1) --max_turns=20 enables fast iteration feedback (~10-15 min) without committing to full 55 turns. (2) First 20 turns sufficient to assess projector sanity (strand stability, token growth patterns, loss distribution). (3) If trajectory SANE after 20 turns, can extend to full 55 in Cycle 52. EXPECTED OUTCOMES: (a) JSON files generated: ./output_tensors/tensor_cycle_*.json (1-20, potentially more if --max_turns not respected). (b) Terminal output per cycle: 'Cycle N: M strands, K key_claims, L losses, Q open_questions'. (c) Final summary (if implemented): token growth chart, strand stability metrics. ASSESSMENT CRITERIA (Cycle 51 post-execution): SANE = stable strands (same titles across cycles), bounded tokens (50-150/cycle), healthy loss distribution (2-5/cycle), grounded claims (T ≥ 0.8), <50% strand churn. PATHOLOGICAL = chaotic strands, unbounded/zero tokens, >10 or <1 losses/cycle, incoherent claims (T <0.6), >50% churn. CYCLE 51-52 NEXT STEPS: IF SANE → activate Phase 1+ metrics (d_strand, d_claim), design Phase 2. IF PATHOLOGICAL → debug (inspect cycle 3-5 JSON + input turns, identify root cause), iterate. IF PARTIAL (e.g., first 10 sane, then fails) → analyze failure threshold, continue analysis on successful subset.

key_claims.text: Smoke test success → PROCEED to Phase 1 full execution: no blockers, infrastructure validated.
Recommended execution: --max_turns=20 for fast iteration (~10-15 min) to assess projector sanity before full 55-turn commit.
SANE trajectory criteria: stable strands, 50-150 tokens/cycle, 2-5 losses/cycle, T≥0.8 claims, <50% churn.
PATHOLOGICAL criteria: chaotic strands, unbounded/zero tokens, extreme losses, T<0.6 claims, >50% churn.
Post-execution path: SANE→Phase 1+ metrics, PATHOLOGICAL→debug cycle 3-5, PARTIAL→analyze failure threshold.

strands.title: CYCLE 51 OPEN QUESTIONS — REFINED AFTER SMOKE TEST SUCCESS
strands.content: CYCLE 51 OPEN QUESTIONS (refined from Cycle 50 after smoke test success): IMMEDIATE (CYCLE 51, FULL EXECUTION STAGE): (1) Does run_projection.py CLI --max_turns flag work? If not, will execute full 55 turns (longer than predicted). (2) Haiku API token consumption: Does actual consumption match token_estimate()? Critical for predicting execution duration. (3) Trajectory SANITY ASSESSMENT (primary question): After first 20 turns, is trajectory SANE (stable strands, bounded tokens, healthy losses) or PATHOLOGICAL (chaotic, unbounded, extreme compression)? (4) Strand title evolution: Do strand titles remain semantically consistent across cycles 1-20? Or do they churn (>50%/cycle)? (5) Loss type distribution: Which loss categories dominate (context_pressure, traversal_bias, authorial_choice, practical_constraint)? Do they correlate with turn count growth? SECONDARY (CYCLE 51-52, IF SANE): (6) Can d_strand distance metric be computed reliably from trajectory? (7) Can d_claim drift metric be computed reliably from trajectory? (8) Do Phase 1+ metrics reveal projector behavior (e.g., compression ratio, strand stability, claim entropy)? TERTIARY (IF PATHOLOGICAL OR PARTIAL FAILURE): (9) What is root cause of pathology? Projector algorithm, Haiku summaries, log_reader, token_estimate? (10) Can single-cycle debug (cycle 3-5) identify root cause and suggest fix? (11) Is fix simple (prompt tuning, loss threshold adjustment) or systemic (projector redesign)? CARRY-FORWARD (UNRESOLVED): Phase 4a sufficiency for other baseline_run* sessions; replay module code review prerequisites (proxy schema, page fault def, pager integration); Phase 2 design objectives.

key_claims.text: Immediate Cycle 51 question: Does run_projection.py --max_turns flag work as expected?
Primary assessment question (Cycle 51): Is Phase 1 trajectory SANE or PATHOLOGICAL after first 20 turns?
Strand stability question: Do strand titles remain consistent across cycles 1-20, or churn unexpectedly?
If trajectory SANE: Phase 1+ metrics (d_strand, d_claim) should be computable from trajectory.
If trajectory PATHOLOGICAL: root cause analysis requires cycle 3-5 JSON inspection + input turn comparison.

open_questions: Does run_projection.py CLI --max_turns flag exist and work correctly? (Will test during Phase 1 execution.)
What is actual Haiku API token consumption vs. token_estimate() prediction? Does it match within ±10%?
After first 20 turns of Phase 1 execution: is trajectory SANE (stable strands, bounded tokens, healthy losses) or PATHOLOGICAL (chaotic, unbounded, extreme)?
Do strand titles remain semantically consistent across cycles 1-20? Or do they churn/drift unexpectedly?
Which loss categories dominate in Phase 1 trajectory (context_pressure, traversal_bias, authorial_choice, practical_constraint)? Do they correlate with cycle progression?
If trajectory SANE: can d_strand and d_claim metrics be reliably computed from cycle-to-cycle deltas?
If trajectory SANE: what do Phase 1+ metrics reveal about projector behavior (compression ratio, strand stability, claim entropy)?
If trajectory PATHOLOGICAL or PARTIAL FAILURE: what is root cause (projector logic, Haiku summaries, log_reader, token_estimate)?
If pathology detected: is fix simple (prompt tuning, threshold adjustment) or systemic (redesign)?
Is Phase 4a log_reader patch (turn 147-148) sufficient for ALL baseline_run* sessions, or might other sessions have different JSONL formats?
Replay module code review: what is exact proxy.py JSONL schema? (Full message snapshots or API boundary markers only?)
Replay module: how is 'page fault' formally defined? (String-match re-request? Semantic detection? Token-level?)
Replay module: what is pager.py integration pattern? (Proxy pipes through pager? Optional?)
Where should final Phase 1 trajectory be stored? (./output_tensors/tensor_cycle_*.json assumed; confirm intended location.)
instructions_for_next:
[
  "CYCLE 51 IMMEDIATE: PHASE 1 FULL EXECUTION — NEXT 15-25 MINUTES",
  "",
  "GOAL: Execute Phase 1 tensor projection on baseline_run1 session (first 20 turns) and assess trajectory sanity.",
  "",
  "STEP 1: PHASE 1 FULL EXECUTION (this turn, ~10-15 min for 20 turns)",
  "  COMMAND:",
  "    cd /home/tony/projects/hamutay",
  "    python -m hamutay.run_projection \\",
  "      /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl \\",
  "      ./output_tensors/ \\",
  "      --max_turns=20",
  "",
  "  EXPECTED OUTPUT (per cycle):",
  "    'Cycle N: M strands, K key_claims, L losses, Q open_questions'",
  "    [Repeat for N=1 to N=20, or until --max_turns limit or session end]",
  "",
  "  JSON OUTPUT:",
  "    ./output_tensors/tensor_cycle_001.json through tensor_cycle_020.json (or higher if --max_turns not enforced)",
  "",
  "  MONITOR FOR ERRORS:",
  "    - Parse error: malformed JSONL, unexpected turn structure → indicates log_reader issue",
  "    - API error: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY invalid, rate limit, auth failure → indicates env/auth issue",
  "    - Token explosion: token_estimate grows >300 per cycle → indicates token_estimate bug",
  "    - Timeout: execution hangs after cycle N → indicates API latency or infinite loop",
  "",
  "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════",
  "",
  "STEP 2: TRAJECTORY SANITY ASSESSMENT (this turn, ~5-10 min after execution completes)",
  "",
  "QUICK HEALTH CHECKS (RED FLAG DETECTION):",
  "",
  "  1. FILE GENERATION",
  "     □ ls -la ./output_tensors/tensor_cycle_*.json",
  "     → Expected: 20-25 JSON files (one per cycle)",
  "     → RED FLAG: <10 files or broken JSON format",
  "",
  "  2. CYCLE COUNT & CONTENT VERIFICATION",
  "     □ for i in 001 005 010 020; do",
  "         head -c 200 ./output_tensors/tensor_cycle_$i.json",
  "       done",
  "     → Expected: Valid JSON with 'strands' key visible",
  "     → RED FLAG: empty files, truncated JSON, missing 'strands' key",
  "",
  "  3. STRAND TITLE EVOLUTION (sample cycles)",
  "     □ python3 -c \"",
  "       import json",
  "       for i in [1, 5, 10, 20]:",
  "           try:",
  "               with open(f'./output_tensors/tensor_cycle_{i:03d}.json') as f:",
  "                   t = json.load(f)",
  "                   titles = [s['title'] for s in t.get('strands', [])]",
  "                   print(f'Cycle {i}: {len(titles)} strands')",
  "                   for title in titles[:3]:",
  "                       print(f'  - {title[:80]}')",
  "           except Exception as e:",
  "               print(f'Cycle {i}: ERROR - {e}')",
  "     \"",
  "     → Expected: consistent strand titles across cycles (same topics), <50% churn per cycle",
  "     → RED FLAG: completely different titles each cycle, strand count >10, no coherence",
  "",
  "  4. TOKEN GROWTH TRAJECTORY",
  "     □ python3 -c \"",
  "       import json",
  "       tokens = []",
  "       for i in range(1, 21):",
  "           try:",
  "               with open(f'./output_tensors/tensor_cycle_{i:03d}.json') as f:",
  "                   t = json.load(f)",
  "                   est = t.get('overall_token_estimate', 0)  # Or similar field",
  "                   tokens.append(est)",
  "           except: pass",
  "       print(f'Token trajectory: {tokens}')",
  "       if tokens:",
  "           print(f'  Min: {min(tokens)}, Max: {max(tokens)}, Mean: {sum(tokens)/len(tokens):.0f}')",
  "     \"",
  "     → Expected: monotonic or stable growth, 50-150 tokens/cycle, final <2000",
  "     → RED FLAG: tokens <10, >300, or chaotic (spiking/collapsing)",
  "",
  "  5. LOSSES PER CYCLE",
  "     □ python3 -c \"",
  "       import json",
  "       for i in [1, 5, 10, 20]:",
  "           try:",
  "               with open(f'./output_tensors/tensor_cycle_{i:03d}.json') as f:",
  "                   t = json.load(f)",
  "                   losses = t.get('declared_losses', [])",
  "                   print(f'Cycle {i}: {len(losses)} losses')",
  "           except: print(f'Cycle {i}: ERROR')",
  "     \"",
  "     → Expected: 2-5 losses per cycle (healthy compression), reasons justified",
  "     → RED FLAG: 0 losses (no compression), >10 losses (too aggressive), unjustified reasons",
  "",
  "  6. CLAIM CONFIDENCE (sample from cycle 5)",
  "     □ python3 -c \"",
  "       import json",
  "       with open('./output_tensors/tensor_cycle_005.json') as f:",
  "           t = json.load(f)",
  "           claims = []",
  "           for s in t.get('strands', []):",
  "               claims.extend(s.get('key_claims', []))",
  "           if claims:",
  "               t_values = [c.get('truth', 0) for c in claims[:10]]",
  "               print(f'Sample claim T values: {t_values}')",
  "               print(f'  Mean: {sum(t_values)/len(t_values):.2f}')",
  "     \"",
  "     → Expected: T values 0.8-0.95 (confident), I values 0.05-0.15 (low uncertainty)",
  "     → RED FLAG: T values <0.6, I values >0.5 (incoherent, uncertain)",
  "",
  "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════",
  "",
  "STEP 3: SANITY DECISION & CLASSIFICATION",
  "",
  "Based on checks 1-6, classify trajectory:",
  "",
  "  SANE TRAJECTORY (all checks ✓):",
  "    ✓ Files generated successfully (20+ JSON files)",
  "    ✓ Valid JSON, 'strands' key present",
  "    ✓ Strand titles consistent across cycles (same topics, <50% churn)",
  "    ✓ Token growth bounded (50-150/cycle, <2000 total)",
  "    ✓ Healthy losses (2-5/cycle, justified reasons)",
  "    ✓ Claim confidence high (T ≥ 0.8, I ≤ 0.15)",
  "",
  "  PATHOLOGICAL TRAJECTORY (any check ✗):",
  "    ✗ File generation failed or broken JSON",
  "    ✗ Strands chaotic (new titles every cycle, no coherence)",
  "    ✗ Token growth unbounded (>300/cycle) or collapsed (<10)",
  "    ✗ Extreme losses (0 or >10/cycle) or unjustified",
  "    ✗ Claim confidence low (T <0.6, I >0.5)",
  "",
  "  PARTIAL SUCCESS (mixed results):",
  "    ◐ First 10 cycles SANE, then fails at cycle 11+",
  "    ◐ OR specific cycles fail while others succeed",
  "    → Analyze failure point: does it correlate with turn count, content type, or token threshold?",
  "",
  "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════",
  "",
  "STEP 4: DECISION TREE — NEXT ACTIONS (Cycle 51-52)",
  "",
  "  IF SANE:",
  "    CYCLE 52 ACTIONS:",
  "    (1) EXTEND EXECUTION: Run full 55 turns (or --max_turns=40) for comprehensive trajectory",
  "    (2) COMPUTE PHASE 1+ METRICS:",
  "        - d_strand: strand distance matrix (cycle N vs cycle N+1)",
  "        - d_claim: claim drift (new/updated/removed claims per cycle)",
  "        - compression_ratio: tokens_in vs tokens_out per cycle",
  "        - loss_category_distribution: which loss types dominate?",
  "    (3) DESIGN PHASE 2 OBJECTIVES: minimal spec doc (2-3 pages) outlining next phase goals",
  "    (4) PARALLEL: Complete replay module code review (pager.py, proxy.py, eval.py)",
  "",
  "  IF PATHOLOGICAL:",
  "    CYCLE 51-52 DEBUG PHASE:",
  "    (1) IDENTIFY FAILURE POINT:",
  "        - At which cycle does pathology emerge (cycle 3, 5, 10)?",
  "        - Does it correlate with turn count, token threshold, or content pattern?",
  "    (2) DEEP INSPECTION (pick cycle N where churn spiked):",
  "        - Load tensor_cycle_N.json + tensor_cycle_N-1.json",
  "        - Compare strand titles, key_claims: what changed unexpectedly?",
  "        - Load corresponding session turns N-2 to N+2 from baseline_run1.jsonl",
  "        - Q: Did input turn content shift dramatically? Or did projector lose coherence?",
  "    (3) HYPOTHESIS FORMATION:",
  "        - Projector mixes unrelated strands? (design issue)",
  "        - Haiku summaries are drifting? (prompt issue)",
  "        - log_reader mangled input? (format issue)",
  "        - Token estimate broken? (causes compression failure)",
  "    (4) TARGETED FIX:",
  "        - If prompt issue: adjust Haiku system prompt (e.g., stricter grounding instructions)",
  "        - If design issue: adjust projector algorithm (e.g., strand merging thresholds)",
  "        - If token estimate issue: calibrate estimate function against actual Haiku counts",
  "    (5) RE-TEST: Re-run with --max_turns=5 to verify fix on small sample",
  "",
  "  IF PARTIAL SUCCESS (e.g., cycles 1-10 SANE, 11-20 PATHOLOGICAL):",
  "    CYCLE 51-52 ANALYSIS:",
  "    (1) IDENTIFY TRANSITION POINT (e.g., cycle 11 where churn spiked)",
  "    (2) ANALYZE FAILURE THRESHOLD:",
  "        - Token count at cycle 10 vs cycle 11? (is there a breaking point?)",
  "        - Content pattern at cycle 11? (did topic shift?))",
  "        - Strand count at cycle 10 vs 11? (explosion?)",
  "    (3) CONTINUE ANALYSIS ON SANE SUBSET (cycles 1-10):",
  "        - Compute Phase 1+ metrics for cycles 1-10 only",
  "        - Assess strand stability, loss distribution, claim drift in sane region",
  "    (4) TARGETED FIX on failure threshold, then extend execution",
  "",
  "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════",
  "",
  "CYCLE 51 CHECKPOINT:",
  "  ✓ Phase 1 smoke tests PASSED (turn 151-153)",
  "  ✓ Phase 1 full execution COMPLETED (turn ?)",
  "  ✓ Trajectory sanity assessment COMPLETED (SANE / PATHOLOGICAL / PARTIAL)",
  "  ✓ Cycle 51-52 path determined: extend execution OR debug pathology OR analyze threshold",
  "",
  "CYCLE 52 CONTINUATION: Based on Cycle 51 assessment, either extend Phase 1 (SANE) or debug + iterate (PATHOLOGICAL/PARTIAL)."
]
